description:
You are acting as a senior database administrator who has been asked to bring hard numbers to a performance-tuning meeting.  
    A log of the current “slow” SQL statements already exists at:

        /home/user/db/slow_query.log

    Your objectives are:

    1. Produce a tab-separated frequency table of every distinct SQL line appearing in the log.  
       • The file must be written to  
         /home/user/db/slow_query_frequency.tsv  
       • Each output line must contain **exactly two columns** in the following order:  
         a) an integer occurrence count (no leading zeroes)  
         b) the literal SQL text (identical to the log line, including the trailing semicolon)  
       • The two columns must be separated by a single <TAB> character only.  
       • The file must be sorted  
            – primarily by the count in descending numeric order, and  
            – for ties, by the full SQL text in ascending ASCII order.  
       • Every line (including the last) must end with a single newline character “\n”.

    2. Create a compact four-line key-value summary file at  

        /home/user/db/slow_query_summary.log

       The exact required format (including capitalisation) is:

            TOTAL_LINES=<number_of_lines_in_original_log>
            TOTAL_UNIQUE_QUERIES=<number_of_distinct_lines>
            TOP_QUERY=<the_most_frequent_SQL_line>
            TOP_QUERY_COUNT=<its_frequency>

       • Do not surround the SQL text with quotes; write it verbatim.  
       • Each line must end with “\n”.  
       • In case of a tie for most-frequent query, the alphabetically first SQL line
         (the same rule used in step 1) must be chosen.

    3. You may use any combination of standard GNU/Linux command-line tools
       available in a typical non-privileged environment (for example: sort, uniq, wc, awk, head, cut, tr, etc.).
       No external programming languages, no temporary root privileges.

    4. When you are done, verify that:
       • /home/user/db/slow_query_frequency.tsv exists and matches the format rules above.  
       • /home/user/db/slow_query_summary.log exists and shows consistent counts.  

    The automated test suite will compare your two output files byte-for-byte, so pay
    close attention to ordering, delimiters and newlines.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing state ---------------------------------------------------- -->
    <!-- Directory structure -->
    - Directory /home/user/db is present and writable by the current user.

    <!-- Original log file -->
    - File: /home/user/db/slow_query.log
      Contents (each item below ends with an LF character 0x0a):

        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE country = 'US';
        UPDATE inventory SET quantity = quantity - 1 WHERE product_id = 42;
        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        UPDATE inventory SET quantity = quantity - 1 WHERE product_id = 42;
        SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE country = 'US';
        DELETE FROM sessions WHERE last_active < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        DELETE FROM sessions WHERE last_active < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE country = 'US';
        INSERT INTO audit_log (event, created_at) VALUES ('login', NOW());
        SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';

      (Total 14 lines.)

    <!-- Expected artefacts AFTER the agent finishes -------------------------- -->
    - File: /home/user/db/slow_query_frequency.tsv
      Expected content (each line terminated by a single LF, last line included):

        6	SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        3	SELECT id, name FROM customers WHERE country = 'US';
        2	DELETE FROM sessions WHERE last_active < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
        2	UPDATE inventory SET quantity = quantity - 1 WHERE product_id = 42;
        1	INSERT INTO audit_log (event, created_at) VALUES ('login', NOW());

    - File: /home/user/db/slow_query_summary.log
      Expected content (each line terminated by a single LF):

        TOTAL_LINES=14
        TOTAL_UNIQUE_QUERIES=5
        TOP_QUERY=SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending';
        TOP_QUERY_COUNT=6
